methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 18 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
HUMAIN is a sovereign-wealth-backed AI & Software company at the Sovereign (PIF) stage, having raised $40B+ in capitalized commitments under CEO Tareq Amin's leadership. The relevant comp universe comprises 18 same-sector peers, including 1 public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and 8 private firms such as Waymo, Figure AI, Scale AI, and Applied Intuition.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to HUMAIN. Despite an adequate universe of 18 AI & Software comparables, zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-valuation-tier peers met threshold requirements for statistical reliability. A triangulation requiring minimum three datapoints could not be satisfied within the narrow filter set, rendering sector-wide median reference uninformative for a sovereign-backed entity of this scale and capital depth.

This valuation remains constrained by the absence of sufficiently tight comparable multiples. The core uncertainty is HUMAIN's capital deployment trajectory and revenue generation relative to its $40B+ capitalized base—a profile without established precedent in the private AI market. Forward valuation work will require either (1) identification of additional sovereign-tier AI platforms with disclosed financials, or (2) bottom-up cash-flow or deployment-velocity modeling tied to specific platform metrics. Public PLTR provides only loose guidance given differing ownership structure and commercialization stage.
